name: H3 (Uber Hexagonal Grid)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Uber; Python bindings to C library

performance.notes: C library bindings; microsecond-level operations; excellent for spatial joins at scale

agent_readiness.notes: Uber's hierarchical hexagonal geospatial indexing system. Divides Earth into hexagonal cells at multiple resolutions (0-15). lat/lon → H3 cell index. Spatial indexing, aggregation, nearest neighbor. Disk neighbor cells. K-ring queries. Used for ride-sharing demand aggregation, delivery zone analysis, spatial ML features. Bindings: Python, JavaScript, Go, Java, R, Rust. C library with Python h3-py wrapper. DuckDB has native H3 extension.

gotchas: Hexagonal cells not perfectly equal area (minor distortion near poles)
Choosing right resolution requires domain knowledge
H3 v3 vs v4 API differences (breaking change)
Not a GIS system — just indexing and spatial joins
best_when: Geospatial feature engineering for ML (location-based features)
Spatial joins and proximity queries at scale
Converting lat/lon data to hierarchical spatial index
summary: H3 is Uber's hierarchical hexagonal geospatial indexing system. Convert lat/lon to hexagonal cell indices at 16 resolution levels. Spatial aggregation, nearest neighbor, and K-ring queries. C library with Python bindings. Excellent for geospatial ML feature engineering, demand aggregation, and location-based analysis. DuckDB has native H3 extension. Used across mobility, logistics, and location analytics.
